events.title: Nam June Paik created sculptures, like this one, for three decades.
events.description: Nam June Paik created sculptures, like this one, for three decades. 🤖

events.title: Throughout his career, Paik experimented with video, performance, and transmission technologies.
events.description: Throughout his career, Paik experimented with video, performance, and transmission technologies.

events.title: The sculptures, which Paik called Robots, consist of video screens set in discar
events.description: The sculptures, which Paik called Robots, consist of video screens set in discarded televisions, radios, and other household goods. Through these robots, Paik expresses a common theme that appears throughout his work: that the future of art and technology lies not in solid things but in temporal communications.

events.title: To Have or To Be? by Erich Fromm (week 1)
events.description: *To Have or To Be?* is one of the seminal books of the second half of the 20th century. His thesis is that two modes of existence struggle for the spirit of humankind: the having mode, which concentrates on material possessions, power, and aggression, and is the basis of the universal evils of greed, envy, and violence; and the being mode, which is based on love, the pleasure of sharing, and in productive activity. "*To Have or To Be?"* is nothing less than a manifesto for a new social and psych

**What to Expect**
This will be a relaxed and welcoming gathering focused specifically on Korean–English conversation practice. No formal lessons — just real interaction, cultural exchange, and meaningful connection.

• 45–60 min – Korean–English conversation

events.title: Spiritually Attacked: Becoming the Calm in the Storm (online)
events.description:
There are moments in life when it feels like everything is coming at you at once.

Heavy emotions. Strange tension. Sudden conflict. Mental noise. Exhaustion. Fear that does not seem to belong to the moment. Sometimes people call it stress. Sometimes they call it burnout. And sometimes, deeper down, it feels spiritual.

This session is for those moments.

In this Shurch gathering, we will explore what people mean when they say they feel “spiritually attacked” and how to respond without collapsin

events.title: How to Prevent Misunderstandings
events.description:
Preventing Misunderstandings

How often have you said or thought “why don’t they understand me?”
As we all know, misunderstandings are very frustrating and can lead to breakdowns in relationships. Whether the relationship is with a significant other, family member, friend or work colleague, relationships are the basis of life.
Join us for a lively discussion on how to prevent misunderstandings.
Discover the three factors that lead to true understanding.

events.title: Word and Object by Quine Week 1
events.description: How does language come to have meaning and can we trust natural language to be a reliable vehicle for truth? For many in the early analytic tradition, the answer to the meaning of language is intimately related to a correspondence theory of truth where one needs to painstakingly tie words to their reference objects in the world. For these philosophers, logic is the correct tool in order to tame natural language and train it to be a reliable vehicle for truth. For Quine (1908-2000), this is simpl

This session is perfect for:

What we’ll cover:

events.title: To Have or To Be? by Erich Fromm (week 2)
events.description: *"To Have or To Be?"* is one of the seminal books of the second half of the 20th century. His thesis is that two modes of existence struggle for the spirit of humankind: the having mode, which concentrates on material possessions, power, and aggression, and is the basis of the universal evils of greed, envy, and violence; and the being mode, which is based on love, the pleasure of sharing, and in productive activity. "*To Have or To Be?"* is nothing less than a manifesto for a new social and psy

—
Collier Schorr, Herbert, Weekend Leave (A Conscript Rated T1), Kirschbaum, 2001. Chromogenic print: sheet, 43 1/2 × 34 3/4 in. (110.5 × 88.3 cm); image: 43 1/2 × 34 3/4 in. (110.5 × 88.3 cm). Whitney Museum of American Art, New York; purchase with funds from the Photography Committee 2002.105. © Collier Schorr. Courtesy 303 Gallery, New York
Collier Sc

events.title: Drawn from one of six “family albums” that Warhol assembled as a personal archiv
events.description:
Drawn from one of six “family albums” that Warhol assembled as a personal archive, the exhibition features posed pictures, candid shots of social events, his home in Montauk, travels in Europe, and even portraits of his dog, Archie (pictured here). Together, these photographs offer an intimate and varied view of Warhol’s world and day-to-day life.
—
Andy Warhol, (Andy Warhol and Archie), 1973, fro

events.title: Calling all Heroes and Space Oddities!
events.description:
Calling all Heroes and Space Oddities!
It’s been eight years since David Bowie left us and returned to his home planet, and we’ve been feeling Under Pressure ever since.
On Friday, January 10, Let’s Dance through these Changes as DJ Steve Reynolds (Party Like It’s 1999) keeps the Stardust alive with a night of Bowie’s biggest hits and deep cuts from his 50-year career.
Ticket link in bio!

events.title: You know what? There’s way too much music to choose from when you’re doing a Dav
events.description: You know what? There’s way too much music to choose from when you’re doing a David Bowie night! Look at all of these albums (and I forgot a couple of box sets too). Join me and @theskint next Friday, January 10th at @littlefieldnyc for Loving the Alien! Ticket link in my bio!

events.title: Friday January 10th our annual David Bowie tribute with @theskint, Loving the Al
events.description: Friday January 10th our annual David Bowie tribute with @theskint, Loving the Alien, returns to @littlefieldnyc! All the best hits, album tracks and deep cuts all night! Get advance tix at the link in our bio!

events.title: Sponsored Ad: Myrtle Avenue Restaurant Week is back! From February 16-25, experi
events.description: Sponsored Ad: Myrtle Avenue Restaurant Week is back! From February 16-25, experience the culinary journey that awaits on Myrtle Avenue. 15 restaurants in Fort Greene and Clinton Hill are offering 3-course, sit-down dining deals at price points to meet every budget. For $20, $30, or $40, taste cuisines from Cambodia to Mexico, Italy to Nepal, and everywhere in between.

events.title: Sponsored Ad: Myrtle Avenue Restaurant Week is back! Continuing thru Mar. 2, 15
events.description: Sponsored Ad: Myrtle Avenue Restaurant Week is back! Continuing thru Mar. 2, 15 restaurants along Myrtle Avenue in Fort Greene and Clinton Hill are offering 3-course, sit-down dining deals for every palate and at price points for every budget. For $25, $35 or $45, savor diverse cuisines from France to Thailand, Cambodia to Nepal, Italy to India, and everywhere in between.

events.title: The beloved NYC bakery is bringing back its annual Cheesecake May from May 1 to
events.description: The beloved NYC bakery is bringing back its annual Cheesecake May from May 1 to 31, and this year’s lineup will make it hard to settle for just one slice. The month-long menu is a maximalist love letter to cream cheese in all its forms, whether folded into babka, piped into buns or baked into Basque-style rounds.

events.title: There will be no new book smell when Audible opens Audible Story House on Friday
events.description: There will be no new book smell when Audible opens Audible Story House on Friday, May 1, bringing a first-of-its-kind “bookless bookstore,” devoted entirely to audio storytelling, to downtown Manhattan.
